subject: Physical Education
chapter: Physical Education and Sports for CWSN

questions.q: What is the term for a condition where a child has difficulty in seeing objects clearly, which is not due to any eye defect?
questions.explanation: Visual impairment is a condition where a person has difficulty in seeing objects clearly, which is not due to any eye defect. Refractive errors are a type of visual impairment.

questions.q: What is the primary goal of the Unified English Braille (UEB) system?
opts: (B) To increase independence in daily activities
(C) To promote literacy among visually impaired children
(D) To facilitate communication among visually impaired individuals
questions.explanation: The primary goal of the Unified English Braille (UEB) system is to promote literacy among visually impaired children.

questions.q: Which of the following sports is not a Paralympic sport?
questions.explanation: Golf is not a Paralympic sport. However, a golf version for visually impaired players exists.

questions.q: What is the term for a device that converts sound into vibrations or electromagnetic waves, which can be detected by a visually impaired person?
questions.explanation: A tactile graphics display is a device that converts sound into vibrations or electromagnetic waves, which can be detected by a visually impaired person.

questions.q: Which of the following is a benefit of using braille?
questions.explanation: Using braille can improve reading speed, increase independence in daily activities, and enhance communication skills.

questions.q: What is the term for a device that helps a visually impaired person to navigate through a physical space?
questions.explanation: A white cane is a device that helps a visually impaired person to navigate through a physical space.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of assistive technology used by visually impaired individuals?
questions.explanation: Screen readers, braille displays, and prosthetic limbs are all types of assistive technology used by visually impaired individuals.

questions.q: What is the term for a condition where a person has difficulty in hearing or communicating due to a physical or intellectual disability?
questions.explanation: Hearing impairment is a condition where a person has difficulty in hearing or communicating due to a physical or intellectual disability.

questions.q: Which of the following sports is a Paralympic sport for visually impaired individuals?
questions.explanation: Athletics, swimming, and cycling are all Paralympic sports for visually impaired individuals.

questions.q: What is the term for a device that helps a visually impaired person to recognize and read braille?
questions.explanation: A braille display is a device that helps a visually impaired person to recognize and read braille.

questions.q: Which of the following is a benefit of using assistive technology?
questions.explanation: Using assistive technology can improve reading speed, increase independence in daily activities, and enhance communication skills.

questions.q: What is the term for a condition where a person has difficulty in seeing objects clearly, which is due to a problem with the eye itself?
questions.explanation: Refractive errors are a type of visual impairment where a person has difficulty in seeing objects clearly, which is due to a problem with the eye itself.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of braille display?
questions.explanation: A refreshable braille display is a type of braille display that can be used to read digital text in braille.

questions.q: What is the term for a device that helps a visually impaired person to navigate through a physical space using sound waves?
questions.explanation: A sound wave navigation device is a device that helps a visually impaired person to navigate through a physical space using sound waves.

questions.q: Which of the following is a benefit of using a screen reader?
opts: (D) Ability to read digital text in braille
questions.explanation: A screen reader is a device that can read digital text aloud, allowing a visually impaired person to read digital text in braille.

questions.q: What is the term for a condition where a person has difficulty in seeing objects clearly, which is due to a problem with the brain rather than the eye?
opts: (D) Visual impairment due to brain damage
questions.explanation: Visual impairment due to brain damage is a type of visual impairment where a person has difficulty in seeing objects clearly, which is due to a problem with the brain rather than the eye.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of assistive technology used by deaf and hard of hearing individuals?
questions.explanation: Screen readers, braille displays, and cochlear implants are all types of assistive technology used by deaf and hard of hearing individuals.
questions.topic: Assistive Technology for Deaf and Hard of Hearing Individuals

questions.q: What is the term for a device that helps a visually impaired person to recognize and read braille by feeling raised dots on the page?
questions.explanation: A Perkins brailler is a device that helps a visually impaired person to recognize and read braille by feeling raised dots on the page.

questions.q: Which of the following is a benefit of using a tactile graphics display?
opts: (D) Ability to read digital text in braille
questions.explanation: A tactile graphics display is a device that can read digital text aloud, allowing a visually impaired person to read digital text in braille.

questions.q: What is the term for a condition where a person has difficulty in seeing objects clearly, which is due to a problem with the retina?
opts: (D) Visual impairment due to retinal damage
questions.explanation: Visual impairment due to retinal damage is a type of visual impairment where a person has difficulty in seeing objects clearly, which is due to a problem with the retina.
